name: Sad Hill Cemetery Loop from Salas de los Infantes
name_original: Colina Triste — circular desde Salas de los Infantes
description: Explore the Sad Hill Cemetery Loop, a 20.8-mile moderate mountain bike trail with cinematic history and scenic views near Salas de los Infan
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Salas de los Infantes are set within the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de la Demanda in Burgos, Spain. The region features varied elevations, with a landscape composed of rebollo oak forests, pine forests, and river valleys like those of the Pedroso and Arlanza rivers. This diverse environment provides a range of natural features suitable for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Salas De Los Infantes The most popular mountain bike trail is Sad Hill Cemetery Loop from Salas de los Infantes, a 20.8 miles (33.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 38 minutes to complete. This route leads through historic landscapes, including the Sad Hill Cemetery area. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cabezón de la Sierra – Hacinas Village loop from Hacinas, a moderate 16.6 miles (26.8 km) path. This trail showcases charming villages and natural scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the Castrovido – Salas de los Infantes loop from Salas de los Infantes, a 29.3 miles (47.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 26 minutes. Mountain biking around Salas De Los Infantes is defined by mountainous terrain, pine forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Salas De Los Infantes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 140 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Salas De Los Infantes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Salas De Los Infantes?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around Salas De Los Infantes, offering a diverse range of experiences for all skill levels. The majority are moderate, with 22 routes, while 7 are considered difficult and 1 is easy.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Salas De Los Infantes?
A: The region is ideal for mountain biking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, but the higher elevations and forested areas, particularly around the Pedroso River or through pine forests like those near Puerto del Manquillo, can offer cooler conditions. Winter riding is possible, but be prepared for colder weather and potentially snow at higher altitudes in the Sierra de la Demanda.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Salas De Los Infantes?
A: Yes, the region offers options suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of the Sierra de la Demanda&lt;/a&gt;, which utilizes an old railway line, provides an easy-to-ride route with gentle slopes and scenic views, making it a great choice for families. While most routes are moderate, the single easy trail available is also a good starting point for less experienced riders.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while mountain biking in Salas De Los Infantes?
A: Mountain biking in Salas De Los Infantes takes you through a rich variety of landscapes. You'll encounter rebollo oak forests, dense pine forests, scrublands, and meadows. The trails often follow river valleys, such as those of the Pedroso and Arlanza rivers, and offer views of mountainous terrain characteristic of the Sierra de la Demanda. Unique geological features and historical sites also add to the scenic diversity.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Salas De Los Infantes are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103344179/sad-hill-cemetery-loop-from-salas-de-los-infantes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill Cemetery Loop from Salas de los Infantes&lt;/a&gt; (33.4 km) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38276028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castrovido – Salas de los Infantes loop from Salas de los Infantes&lt;/a&gt; (47.1 km). These loops offer convenient starting points and varied terrain.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The region is rich in landmarks. You can visit the reconstructed &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill Cemetery&lt;/a&gt;, famous from 'The Good, the Bad and the Ugly,' which is part of a popular mountain bike loop. Other notable points include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1752092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Manquillo&lt;/a&gt; mountain pass, the charming village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7840361" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbadillo de Herreros&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of the Sierra de la Demanda&lt;/a&gt;. You might also spot impressive rock formations like those at 'Las Calderas'.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Salas De Los Infantes?
A: The mountain bike trails around Salas De Los Infantes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic beauty of the Sierra de la Demanda, and the well-maintained routes that cater to various skill levels. The historical and natural landmarks along the trails are also frequently highlighted as enjoyable features.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, Salas De Los Infantes offers 7 difficult mountain bike routes. These routes typically involve longer distances, significant elevation gains (over 3,000 ft), and require very good fitness and advanced riding skills. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1752092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Manquillo&lt;/a&gt;, for example, features an 11-kilometer climb with gradients up to 11% through pine forests, providing a demanding experience.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that pass through villages or offer amenities?
A: Many routes in the region connect or pass near charming villages. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38249565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezón de la Sierra – Hacinas Village loop from Hacinas&lt;/a&gt; showcases local villages and natural scenery. These villages often provide opportunities for refreshments, cafes, or accommodation, allowing for breaks during your ride.

Q: Are there any routes that follow old railway lines?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of the Sierra de la Demanda&lt;/a&gt; is a varied bike tour that utilizes an old railway line. This route offers a unique riding experience with gentle slopes and excellent views, making it accessible and enjoyable for many cyclists.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail in the area?
A: One of the longer moderate trails is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31446332" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de Castrovido loop from Río Ciruelos&lt;/a&gt;, covering 52.2 km. This route offers an extensive ride through varied terrain, ideal for those looking for a longer day out on the trails.
tips: A work of cinematographic architecture built for the filming of the final scene of the film "Il buono, il brutto, il cattivo" ("The good, the bad and the ugly") in 1966. After 49 years of neglect, in 2015 they began the recovery work of the cemetery at the hands of volunteers from the Sad Hill Cultural Association.

Villanueva de Carazo is a charming town located at an altitude of 1,064 meters. The town has less than 30 inhabitants and a calm and welcoming atmosphere. Its cobbled streets and traditional stone houses reflect its historic charm. In addition, it is surrounded by beautiful natural landscapes.

Salas de los Infantes is the capital of the Sierra de la Demanda region and is located in the valley of the Arlanza river. The origin of the town dates back to the 10th century. Among its main attractions are the church of Santa María, church of Santa Cecilia, the palace of the Infantes de Lara and Plaza Mayor.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered a moderate mountain bike ride. It's best suited for riders with good fitness and advanced riding skills, as some sections can be challenging, corresponding to an STS S2 rating.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, featuring rocky and dusty sections with potholes, steep dirt tracks, and areas with bleached, dry hills, especially around the Sad Hill Cemetery. You might also encounter twisty tarmac sections, wet roads, and areas with pine cone needles within dense forested surroundings.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the loop?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the iconic Sad Hill Cemetery, the filming location for 'The Good, the Bad and the Ugly'. You'll also pass by the charming Church of Villanueva de Carazo and the historic center of Salas de los Infantes, which is a highlight in itself: Salas de los Infantes Historic Center.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sad Hill Cemetery Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends in Salas de los Infantes. You can typically find parking within the town, especially near the Salas de los Infantes Historic Center, which serves as a convenient trailhead for this route.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on this trail. However, as parts of the route pass through natural areas and potentially near livestock, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to ensure their safety and respect local wildlife and other trail users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this loop?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for mountain biking this loop. The weather is usually milder, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be hot and dry, while winters might bring colder temperatures and potentially wet or muddy conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this route or the Parque Natural Sabinares de Arlanza-La Yecla?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a specific permit to ride this mountain bike loop, nor are there any entrance fees for the trail itself. The route passes through the Parque Natural Sabinares de Arlanza-La Yecla, which is freely accessible, though visitors are expected to respect natural regulations and guidelines.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other paths. Notably, it covers about 43% of the 'Etapa 8 Santo Domingo de Silos - Salas de los Infantes' and 10% of the 'Etapa 7 Covarrubias - Santo Domingo de Silos'. You'll also briefly cross parts of the 'Camino de Contreras a La Revilla' and 'Sendero de Carazo a Villanueba de Carazo'.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, and sun protection. A GPS device or a downloaded map is also highly recommended, as some sections might be less clearly marked.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The trail traverses a breathtaking valley setting near Santo Domingo de Silos, offering picturesque views. Beyond the unique Sad Hill Cemetery, the natural beauty of the valley and the Sierra de la Demanda provides many scenic opportunities.

faqs.question: Is there any specific local advice for riding this trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail is moderate, be prepared for potentially rocky and dusty sections, especially leading to Sad Hill Cemetery. If you're a fan of 'The Good, the Bad and the Ugly', allow extra time at the cemetery to explore the reconstructed set and its unique atmosphere. Starting early in the day is advisable, especially during warmer months, to avoid the midday heat.

waypoints.name: Salas de los Infantes Historic Center
